mnemonics: I/O B.U.S. (Interrupts Over Bus Utilization & Synchronization): A system bus is used to handle multiple devices, where Interrupts, Bus arbitration, and Synchronization are key.
keyPoints: Multiple devices are connected to and communicate with the processor via a shared system bus.
Bus arbitration is the process of deciding which device gets control of the bus when multiple devices want to use it simultaneously.
Interrupts are signals from I/O devices to the processor indicating they need attention, providing an efficient alternative to polling.
commonMistakes: Confusing bus arbitration (controlling bus access) with interrupt handling (signaling the CPU).
Assuming that a device with a higher priority in a daisy-chain arbitration will always win the bus, which is only true for its priority level.
